rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Motion For Protective Order And Appointment Of A Discovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 28, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS Joinder To Motion For Protective Order And Appointment Of A Discovery Referee- Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 28, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 5, Plaintiff American Marketing Complex, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Answer Or In The Alternative To Strike Affirmative Defenses, As Discovery Sanctions For For Monetary Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. Defendant Cardiac Network, Inc's answer is struck for failure to comply with the Court's order of December 20, 2011. Monetary sanctions are awarded in favor of Plaintiff in the amount of $1,250. rulings.Case Title: BENITO CARRERA et al VS. TRACTEL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, For Improper Service And/Or On The Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens; Memo P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 28, 2012, Line 10. CROSS DEFENDANT RIGHT ANGLE METAL FABRICATORS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, For Improper Service And/Or On The Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens. The motion to dismiss the cross-complaint is granted. California is a seriously inconvenient forum to adjudicate the cross-complaint and Canada is a suitable alternative forum. Tractel has already filed an action against cross-defendant Right Angle in Canada. Right Angle is subject to Canadian jurisdiction and there is no statute of limitations problem given that the underlying tort case is still being litigated. Neither Tractel nor Right Angle are California residents and their dealings with each other occurred in Canada. California has little to no interest in how the indemnification issue is resolved. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NADER SHABAHANNGI VS. 124 HAIGHT STREET, LLC, CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Appointment Of A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 28, 2012, line 16, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER. Denied. Plaintiff has not shown that the appointment of a receiver is either necessary or appropriate under Code of Civil Procedure section 564. Based on Defendant's representation in his opposition papers that he intends to continue to apply all rent payments received from 124-126 Haight Street to the payment of real property taxes and mortgages on the properties, he is required to do so pending final resolution of this case or other court order to the contrary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
